mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% indicates tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 7.5% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and GDP suggests strong supply-side momentum, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in high INDPRO/GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates above 4.5%, compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending, as indicated by increasing residential construction permits, signals strong demand-side momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 for any 1-year window, or 5-day returns in high HOUST periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with declining unemployment signals robust labor market conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20-day returns in high PAYEMS/low UNRATE periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high RSAFS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during a supply shock reduces corporate output and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of instances where INDPRO falls >2% despite the signal.
